title: Google Summer of Code 2025
description: Google Summer of Code is a global program focused on bringing more developers into open source software development. GSoC contributors work with an open source organization on a 12+ week programming project during their free time.
eligibility: Must be 18 years or older. Open to students, recent graduates, and beginners in open source. Must be eligible to work in your country.

title: MLH Fellowship - Open Source
description: The MLH Fellowship is a remote internship alternative for software engineers. Spend 12 weeks building your skills by contributing to Open Source projects used by companies around the world.
eligibility: Students and recent graduates. Must be able to dedicate 20-30 hours per week.

title: Outreachy Internships
description: Outreachy provides internships in open source and open science. Outreachy internships are for people subject to systemic bias and underrepresentation in the technical industry.
eligibility: For people subject to systemic bias and underrepresentation in tech. Must meet contribution requirements.

title: Google STEP Internship
description: Student Training in Engineering Program (STEP) is a developmental internship at Google for first and second-year undergraduate students with a passion for technology.
eligibility: First or second year undergraduate students. Pursuing a bachelor's degree in Computer Science or related field.

title: Microsoft Explore Internship
description: Explore is a 12-week summer internship program specifically designed for college underclassmen (first and second-year students) and is dedicated to growing the next generation of technologists.
eligibility: Pursuing Bachelor's degree (first or second year). Computer Science, Computer Engineering, or related major.

title: Meta University Program
description: Meta University is a 12-week internship program at Meta that provides a hands-on technical project experience for freshmen and sophomores who are interested in engineering or product management.
eligibility: Freshman or sophomore students. Pursuing a degree in CS, CE, or related STEM field.

title: Y Combinator Startup School
description: Startup School is a free online program and global community for founders. The curriculum covers everything from coming up with startup ideas to growing a company.
eligibility: Open to anyone working on or interested in starting a startup.

title: Kleiner Perkins Fellows
description: The Kleiner Perkins Fellows program connects ambitious college students with top technology startups for the summer. Fellows spend 10 weeks at a KP portfolio company.
eligibility: Undergraduate and graduate students in engineering, design, or product management.

title: Interact Fellowship
description: Interact is a community of the most talented young technologists and entrepreneurs. Fellows connect with founders, investors, and innovators at the forefront of technology.
eligibility: Students and recent graduates with demonstrated interest and talent in technology.

title: Gates Scholarship
description: The Gates Scholarship is a highly selective, full scholarship for exceptional minority high school seniors to maximize their impact in college and beyond.
eligibility: US citizen minority students. Pell Grant eligible. Minimum 3.3 GPA. Senior in high school.

title: Chevening Scholarship
description: Chevening is the UK government's international awards programme aimed at developing global leaders. It offers scholarships and fellowships to study in the UK.
eligibility: Citizens of eligible countries. 2+ years of work experience. Bachelor's degree holder.

title: Fulbright U.S. Student Program
description: The Fulbright U.S. Student Program provides grants for US citizens to pursue graduate study, research, or English Teaching Assistant positions internationally.
eligibility: US citizens. Bachelor's degree by time grant begins. Language proficiency may be required.

title: MLH Hackathon - Global Hack Week
description: MLH Global Hack Week is a series of beginner-friendly hackathons with daily challenges, workshops, and prizes. Perfect for first-time hackers.
eligibility: Open to all students and developers. Beginners welcome.

title: HackMIT 2025
description: HackMIT is an annual hackathon run by MIT students. Over 1,000 of the most creative, technical, and innovative student hackers from around the world come together to build creative projects.
eligibility: College students from accredited institutions worldwide.

title: TreeHacks at Stanford
description: TreeHacks is Stanford's annual hackathon. Hundreds of hackers come together to build innovative solutions, learn new technologies, and make lifelong connections.
eligibility: College students from any institution. Applications reviewed on rolling basis.

title: Devpost Global AI Hackathon
description: Build AI-powered applications using the latest machine learning models and APIs. Open to teams worldwide with amazing prizes and recognition.
eligibility: Open to all developers worldwide. Solo or teams of up to 5.

title: MIT $100K Entrepreneurship Competition
description: The MIT $100K Entrepreneurship Competition is the largest student-run business plan competition in the world and one of the most prestigious.
eligibility: At least one team member must be a current MIT student, recent graduate, or enrolled staff.

title: Techstars Startup Weekend
description: Startup Weekend is a 54-hour event where developers, designers, marketers, product managers, and startup enthusiasts come together to share ideas, form teams, build products, and launch startups.
eligibility: Open to anyone interested in entrepreneurship and startups.

title: GitHub Octernships
description: GitHub Octernships connects students with industry partners for real-world software engineering roles. Build production-level software and get paid while in school.
eligibility: Currently enrolled students. Must have GitHub account and basic coding knowledge.

title: Amazon Future Engineer Scholarship
description: Amazon Future Engineer Scholarship supports high school seniors with financial need who plan to major in computer science. Recipients also receive a guaranteed summer internship at Amazon.
eligibility: High school senior. Plan to major in CS. Family income below $65,000.

title: NASA Space Apps Challenge
description: The NASA International Space Apps Challenge is an annual hackathon that takes place over 48 hours in cities around the world. Participants collaborate on challenges using NASA's open data.
eligibility: Open to anyone. No prior space or programming experience needed.

title: Hugging Face ML Fellowship
description: The Hugging Face ML Fellowship is a program that provides selected candidates with mentorship, compute resources, and guidance to work on machine learning research and open-source projects.
eligibility: Students and early-career researchers with strong ML background.

title: Jane Street INSIGHT Program
description: INSIGHT is a free educational program in which participants spend a week at Jane Street's NYC office learning about trading, programming, and mathematical modeling.
eligibility: Undergraduate women and non-binary students in CS, math, or related fields.

title: Open Source Initiative Deepdive Grant
description: The OSI provides grants to open source contributors to work on impactful projects. Recipients receive funding and mentorship from the open source community.
eligibility: Anyone contributing to open source projects. Demonstrated contribution history required.

title: Stripe Open Source Retreat
description: The Stripe Open Source Retreat is a 3-month funded sabbatical for open source developers to work on impactful projects that improve the developer ecosystem.
eligibility: Experienced developers with a track record in open source. Proposal for specific project required.

title: Codeforces Educational Round Hackathon
description: A competitive programming hackathon focused on algorithmic problem solving. Participants solve algorithmic challenges over 48 hours to win cash prizes and recognition.
eligibility: Open to all competitive programmers worldwide.

title: NVIDIA Inception Program
description: NVIDIA Inception is a program designed to nurture startups revolutionizing industries with AI. Members receive NVIDIA technology, go-to-market support, and access to NVIDIA experts.
eligibility: Early-stage startups working with AI/ML technologies.

title: Protocol Labs Research Grant
description: Protocol Labs funds research across distributed systems, cryptography, and decentralized networks. Grants support independent researchers and academic teams.
eligibility: Researchers and academics working in distributed systems, cryptography, or Web3.

title: ETHGlobal Online Hackathon
description: ETHGlobal hosts Ethereum hackathons worldwide. Build innovative applications on Ethereum and Web3 infrastructure for large prize pools.
eligibility: Open to all developers. Beginner to advanced.

title: Databricks Lakehouse Scholarship
description: Databricks offers scholarships for students pursuing data engineering, data science, or AI careers. Includes mentorship from Databricks engineers.
eligibility: Undergraduate or graduate students in data science, CS, or related fields.

title: a16z Future Leaders Program
description: a16z Future Leaders is a program for ambitious students and young professionals who want to build the next generation of technology companies.
eligibility: Students and professionals under 30 with demonstrated entrepreneurial spirit.

title: Palantir Scholarship for Women in Tech
description: Palantir's Women in Technology scholarship provides financial support and a summer internship opportunity to outstanding women pursuing degrees in technical fields.
eligibility: Women pursuing Bachelor's or Master's in CS, engineering, or math. US or UK institutions.

title: Shopify Developer Internship
description: Shopify offers 4-month internship terms for developers to work on real products used by millions of merchants worldwide. Interns are treated like full employees with full ownership.
eligibility: Students or recent graduates in any program. Strong coding skills required.

title: Ycombinator W26 Batch
description: Y Combinator invests in early-stage startups twice a year. Founders go through a 3-month program and receive funding, mentorship, and access to the YC network.
eligibility: Early-stage startups. Solo founders or teams. Any background.

title: CodeDay Hackathon
description: CodeDay is a nationwide hackathon for students who don't usually code. No experience needed! Learn from mentors, build a project, and get inspired.
eligibility: Middle school, high school, and college students. Complete beginners welcome.

title: Snap Research Fellowship
description: The Snap Research Fellowship supports PhD students doing cutting-edge research in computer vision, AR/VR, machine learning, and HCI.
eligibility: Current PhD students. Must be doing research in relevant areas.

title: AI for Good Hackathon
description: The AI for Good Global Summit hackathon challenges participants to use artificial intelligence to address the world's most pressing problems.
eligibility: Open to all developers, researchers, and entrepreneurs globally.

title: Mozilla Technology Fund
description: The Mozilla Technology Fund supports open-source projects that promote internet health, privacy, and an open, accessible web for all.
eligibility: Open source projects and independent researchers. Must align with Mozilla's mission.

title: Facebook Hackathon - Developer Circles
description: Facebook Developer Circles hackathon challenges developers to build apps using Meta's developer platforms including React, PyTorch, and WhatsApp APIs.
eligibility: Developers 18 and older. Any experience level.

title: Apple WWDC Scholarship
description: Apple awards scholarships to students and STEM organizations to attend the Worldwide Developers Conference. Scholarship recipients also receive one year of membership in the Apple Developer Program.
eligibility: Enrolled in an accredited academic institution or STEM organization. Must submit a Swift Playgrounds project.
prize: Free WWDC ticket + Apple Developer Program

title: Cloudflare Research Internship
description: Cloudflare Research internship focuses on cutting-edge work in networking, cryptography, distributed systems, and internet infrastructure.
eligibility: PhD students or advanced undergraduates with research experience.

title: Jane Goodall Scholars Program
description: The Jane Goodall Scholars Program supports students committed to using technology and innovation for environmental conservation and sustainability.
eligibility: Undergraduate students with a passion for environmental science and technology.

title: TikTok Developer Hackathon
description: Build creative applications using TikTok's APIs and developer platform. Compete for large prize pools and potential investment.
eligibility: Developers 18+ worldwide. Solo or team (up to 4).

title: AWS Startups - Activate Program
description: AWS Activate provides startups with low-cost, scalable infrastructure via free AWS credits, along with technical support and training resources.
eligibility: Early-stage startups. Must be funded by an investor or be a current startup in an incubator.

title: DeepMind Scholarship
description: DeepMind Scholarship supports exceptional postgraduate students studying artificial intelligence and machine learning at partner universities.
eligibility: Postgraduate students at partner UK universities. Must be studying AI/ML related programs.

title: Remix OSS Contributor Grant
description: Remix awards grants to significant contributors to the Remix web framework. Get paid to improve the open-source tooling used by thousands of developers.
eligibility: Active contributors to the Remix GitHub repository. Strong TypeScript/React skills.

title: OpenAI Residency Program
description: The OpenAI Residency is a 6-month research program for people looking to transition into AI research. Residents work directly with OpenAI researchers on frontier AI projects.
eligibility: Strong programming skills. Background in math, physics, or related technical field. No ML experience required.

title: Anthropic Research Engineer Internship
description: Anthropic internships offer the opportunity to work on cutting-edge AI safety research alongside world-class researchers. Interns contribute directly to Claude and AI safety projects.
eligibility: Graduate students or final-year undergraduates in CS, ML, or related fields.

title: Civic Digital Fellowship
description: The Civic Digital Fellowship places undergraduate and graduate students in federal agencies to use their technology and data skills to improve government services.
eligibility: Undergraduate and graduate students with CS, data science, or design skills. US citizenship required.

title: Buildspace Nights & Weekends
description: Buildspace Nights & Weekends is a 6-week program where ambitious hackers build their dream project alongside a global community of builders.
eligibility: Open to anyone with an idea to build. Any skill level welcome.
